okees-healthy-gray-corned-beef-brisket-f.name: O'kee's Healthy Gray Corned Beef Brisket From Scratch

ingredients.prep: halved lengthwise, and cut into 1/2-inch strips
halved lengthwise, and cut into 1/2-inch strips

instructions: Make brine: Bring water to a boil in a large pot. Stir in salt and sugar until dissolved; remove from heat and add cinnamon stick pieces, juniper berries, cloves, allspice berries, mustard seeds, peppercorns, ginger, and crumbled bay leaves. Let brine cool to room temperature, at least 1 hour.

Prepare corned beef: Place brisket in a large ceramic or glass dish deep enough to keep brisket submerged in brine. Pour brine over brisket; weigh it down to keep submerged. Cover the dish tightly with plastic wrap. Brine brisket in the refrigerator, checking brine level every day, 7 to 10 days.
Remove brisket from brine and rinse under cool running water; discard brine. Place brisket in a large slow cooker; add enough cool water to cover brisket by several inches and season with sea salt.

Cook on High for 2 hours. Add celery root, red potatoes, rutabagas, carrots, parsnips, and onions. Cook on Low until vegetables are softened and brisket is hot and gray in the center, about 30 minutes. An instant-read thermometer inserted into the center of brisket should read 71°C (70 degrees C).
Remove brisket to a cutting board and cover tightly with aluminum foil.
Arrange cabbage wedges into the slow cooker; continue cooking until all vegetables are tender, 20 to 30 minutes.
Slice corned beef into thin slices and serve with vegetables.

oatmeal-chocolate-chip-lactation-cookies.name: Oatmeal Chocolate Chip Lactation Cookies
instructions: Preheat the oven to 177°C (175 degrees C).
Stir water and flaxseed meal together in a small bowl. Let stand until thickened, about 5 minutes.
Beat butter and both sugars with an electric mixer in a large bowl until creamy. Add eggs, one at a time, beating well after each addition. Add flax mixture and vanilla; mix until well combined.

Stir flour, yeast, baking soda, and salt together in a bowl. Mix dry ingredients into the wet ingredients until well combined. Stir in oats, chocolate chips, and honey. Drop spoonfuls of dough 2 inches apart onto ungreased baking sheets.
Bake in the preheated oven until edges are golden, 12 to 15 minutes.
Remove cookies from the oven and transfer to wire racks to cool.

octopus-with-honey-argiro.name: Octopus With Honey
instructions: Clean the octopus: remove the mouth from the center, flip the hood, empty the interiors, wash well and drain. Cut it into pieces of large bite size.
Clean the onions and cross them.
In a wide shallow pot, heat the olive oil over medium heat and add the onions and garlic.
Sauté for about 10 minutes, stringing regularly, until slightly golden, soften and becomes transparent.
Add the octopus, turn the heat on and sauce for 5 minutes, stringing freshly.
Once changed, add the balsamic vinegar, wine and cognac.
Add the tomato crucified and add it to the pot along with the laurel, the spice and a lot of freshly ground pepper.
Once the boat starts, lower the fire. Cover and simmer for about 11⁄2 hours.
To the end of cooking, add the honey and mix gently.
Serve the octopus warm, accompanied by crispy crispy potatoes or French fries.

olive-paste-sandwich-recipe-lezzet.name: Olive Paste Sandwich Recipe

ingredients.item: 2 slices full wheat or veccedil;avdar bread
2 tablespoons of black or green olive crush (pose or homemade)
1-2 slices white cheese or bake cheese (optional)

instructions: 1. Light-and-gray the bread slices;e fry rshâle (optional).
2. Spread the fried or fresh bread slices uniform to Crush olive oil.
3. Place cheese slices, marul or roka leaves, tomato and cucumber slices on the car.
4. Depending on the request, you can sprinkle the pepper or pepper.
5. Close to the other bread slice u close to the car and press the light.
6. Serve by slitting.
7.  Afiyet olsun.

ollie-beans-coconut-bar-cookies.name: Ollie Bean's Coconut Bar Cookies
instructions: Preheat the oven to 177°C (175 degrees C). Grease an 8x12-inch baking pan.
Beat butter, 1/2 cup brown sugar, and 1 cup flour together in a bowl using an electric mixer until well blended. Press into the prepared pan.
Bake in the preheated oven until firm but not browned, about 20 minutes. Remove from oven and reduce temperature to 163°C (165 degrees C).

Beat 1 cup brown sugar, eggs, 2 tablespoons flour, vanilla extract, baking powder, and salt together in a bowl using an electric mixer until mixture is light, about 2 minutes. Add walnuts; beat until blended but not pureed. Spread mixture over the crust; sprinkle with coconut.
Bake in the preheated oven until set, about 25 minutes. Cut while warm.

ombre-christmas-tree-cookies.name: Ombre Christmas Tree Cookies

ingredients.item: Sanding sugar in 3 shades of green and gold dragees
Sanding sugar in 3 shades of green and gold dragees

instructions: Position racks in upper and lower thirds of oven; preheat to 177°C. Line 2 rimmed baking sheets with parchment paper.

Generously flour a rolling pin and work surface. Working with 1 disc at a time, roll dough to 1/4" thick. Cut out as many cookies as possible with cookie cutters (if dough becomes too soft, chill until firm). Place cookies 1/2" apart on prepared baking sheets. Chill 15 minutes.
Bake cookies until edges are light golden brown, 10–12 minutes. Transfer to wire racks and let cool.

Using small offset spatula, frost trees with icing. While still wet, decorate each cookie in 3 sections to create an ombre effect—start by covering bottom third with darkest green sanding sugar. Sprinkle the medium green shade in the center, then the light green shade over the top third. Tap cookie lightly to remove excess sprinkles. Top tips of trees with gold dragees to look like a tree topper. Let icing dry before storing cookies, about 3 hours.
Cookies can be made 10 days ahead; store in an airtight container at room temperature.

omelette.name: Omelette
instructions: In a medium bowl, beat the eggs.
Melt the butter in a medium frying pan over medium heat.

Pour in the beaten eggs. Stir and shake the pan, moving the eggs around until a few curds form. Reduce the heat to low, then flatten the eggs until the still-runny egg covers the entire bottom of the pan. Add any fillings that you would like.
Let the omelette cook until the eggs are fully set, 1-2 minutes, then gently roll the omelette and transfer to a plate. The surface should be smooth with no color.
Sprinkle with chives, if desired.
Enjoy!

one-pan-chicken-and-broccoli-stir-fry.name: One-pan Chicken And Broccoli Stir-fry
instructions: Make the stir-fry sauce: In a small bowl, mix together the soy sauce, honey, garlic, ginger, and sesame seeds.
Make the stir fry: Heat the canola oil oil in a large nonstick pan over medium-high heat. Season with the salt and pepper, then stir until the chicken is browned, about 5 minutes.

Pour the sauce into pan and stir to coat the chicken, then bring the sauce to a boil. Once the sauce is bubbling, add the broccoli and red bell pepper to the pan and toss to coat in the sauce. Cook until the chicken is cooked through and the vegetables are tender, 5-8 minutes more.
Serve over rice, if desired.
Enjoy!

orange-turkey-recipe-comedera.name: Orange Turkey Recipe
orange-turkey-recipe-comedera.original_name: Receta de pavo a la naranja

ingredients.item: 2 cdas ralladura de piel de naranja

instructions: Inicia la receta y toma las pechugas o solomillos de pavo. Condimenta con sal y pimienta al gusto.
Warm a frying pan or grill with olive oil and seal the turkey for about 7 minutes on each side.
While the turkey's cooking, place an orange juice, fruit frying, sugar, mustard, fresh rosemary and soya sauce in a saucer.
Mezcla y cocina a fuego lento por 4 minutos. Corta en tiras delgadas la cebolla y agrega a la mezcla. Termina de cocinar hasta que se forme una salsa medio glaseada. Apaga y reserva.
The breasts have to be dyed but juicy inside. Once ready, remove from the frying pan and cut onto the surface.
Put your breast in a plate and have a bath with an orange sauce until you get well impregnated.
Now, you're ready to taste.
Enjoy!

oven-baked-beef-stew.name: Oven-baked Beef Stew

ingredients.item: Salt and freshly ground black pepper to taste

instructions: Preheat the oven to 149°C (150 degrees C).

Pour flour into a shallow bowl and turn beef in flour, shaking meat to remove excess flour. Heat oil in an ovenproof casserole dish over high heat. Brown beef cubes in hot oil in batches on all sides, about 5 minutes per batch. Transfer beef to a plate.

Add onion, celery, and carrots to the casserole dish, reduce heat to medium, and cook until softened, about 5 minutes. Add garlic and cook for 1 minute. Pour in broth. Return beef to the casserole dish and stir in bay leaves. Cover casserole.
Bake in the preheated oven until beef is tender, 1 1/2 to 2 hours. Remove bay leaves before serving and season with salt and pepper.

oven-roasted-tri-tip.name: Oven-roasted Tri-tip
instructions: Whisk oil, soy sauce, cumin, garlic, and pepper together in a large glass or ceramic bowl. Add tri-tip and onion; toss to evenly coat. Cover the bowl with plastic wrap and marinate in the refrigerator for 2 hours, or up to 24 hours.
Preheat the oven to 218°C (220 degrees C). Remove tri-tip mixture from refrigerator; let rest at room temperature while preheating the oven.
Remove tri-tip from marinade and shake off excess; place on a rack in a shallow roasting pan. Remove onions from marinade; place around tri-tip. Discard remaining marinade.
Roast in the preheated oven until an instant-read thermometer inserted into the center reads 57°C (57 degrees C) for medium-rare or 66°C (66 degrees C) for medium, 30 to 45 minutes.
Allow to rest at least 15 minutes before slicing against the grain.

oysters-with-brown-sugarchipotle-butter.name: Oysters With Brown Sugar–chipotle Butter

ingredients.prep: Well Fleet, or Malpeque), freshly shucked, on the half shell, with liquor

instructions: Mix garlic, chile, butter, bourbon, and brown sugar in a small bowl until smooth; season with salt and pepper.

Heat broiler. Spread a layer of kosher salt on a rimmed baking sheet (about 2 cups) and arrange oysters on top (the salt will prevent them from tipping over). Place about 1/2 tsp. butter mixture on each oyster.
Broil oysters until butter is melted and sugar is beginning to caramelize, about 3 minutes.
DO AHEAD: Butter can be made 2 days ahead. Cover and chill.
